message:
fix(rchd): improve E2E test stability with exponential backoff

Refactors the daemon socket communication in E2E tests to be more
resilient under heavy system load, reducing flaky test failures.

Changes:
- Extract send_socket_request_once() for single-attempt requests
- Refactor send_socket_request() to use exponential backoff:
  - Start at 10ms, double each retry, cap at 500ms
  - Total retry window of ~5 seconds
  - Much more adaptive than fixed 50ms delays
- Add wait_for_daemon_ready() to verify daemon health before tests:
  - Checks actual health endpoint response, not just socket connectivity
  - Daemon can be connectable while still initializing
- Update test_daemon_verbose_mode to use health-based readiness check

The previous fixed-delay approach (5 retries x 50ms) was insufficient
on heavily loaded CI runners where daemon startup could exceed 250ms.
Exponential backoff provides fast responses when daemon is ready while
tolerating slow startups without hard-coding long waits.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
